44.reason: The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of items read, not -1 on end-of-file. The input ends with -1 as a value, not EOF, so this loop will never terminate correctly. It should check if n == -1 after reading.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition should check for -1 as the end-of-operations marker, but scanf returns the number of items read, not the value. The correct condition should be `while(scanf("%d",&n) == 1 && n != -1)` to properly detect the end of input.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Loop condition should check n != -1 to stop on -1 input; scanf returns 1, not -1, so loop does not terminate when -1 is read, but only on EOF. Works for this specific input but is logically incorrect.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition is incorrect. scanf returns the number of successfully read items, not the value read. The logic `scanf(...) != -1` will likely result in an infinite loop or incorrect termination because scanf returns 1 on success, not the value read.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  When pop() returns due to an empty stack (error case), it does not return a value, so `out` is uninitialized.
